---
title: "Document Status Types"
---

When a batch is transferred to the Hyland Experience Capture Verify Data interface for further processing, each document inside the batch has a current status which may be different from that of the other documents in the batch.

The status of a document depends on various parameters, such as:

-
whether a default or custom document class is assigned to the document

-
whether the class assigned requires modification or confirmation, and

-
whether the document metadata fields require manual verification or not.

The name of the default document class may vary based on the Content Queue to which the document is mapped.

The following table describes the different types of document classes that are available. The class name "AP Invoice" is used as an example to indicate the high confidence status types.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

![](data:image/gif;base64,)

| Default Class Assigned | Need to change or confirm document class? | Need to verify metadata fields |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Yes | Yes |
| The assigned class appears in black bold font with a red eye beside it. | No | Yes |
| The assigned class with verified fields appears in black bold font with a green tick beside it. | No | No |
